title: Acute promyelocytic leukaemia: recognising PML::RARA as a haematological emergency
learning_objectives: Recognise classic laboratory features of acute promyelocytic leukaemia
Integrate morphology, flow cytometry and rapid molecular testing
Understand the urgency of initiating ATRA when APL is suspected
clinical_vignette: A 36-year-old woman presents with gingival bleeding, epistaxis and extensive bruising. She is haemodynamically stable but has laboratory evidence of disseminated intravascular coagulation. Peripheral blood and bone marrow are reviewed urgently.

morphology.peripheral_blood: Abnormal promyelocytes with heavy granulation and multiple Auer rods; occasional bundles ('faggot cells').
morphology.bone_marrow: Marrow extensively infiltrated by abnormal promyelocytes.

flow_cytometry.interpretation: Typical immunophenotype of acute promyelocytic leukaemia.

cytogenetics: Rapid FISH detects PML::RARA fusion consistent with t(15;17)(q24;q21).
molecular_genetics: RT-PCR confirms PML::RARA transcript. FLT3 mutation testing pending.
integrated_laboratory_diagnosis: Acute promyelocytic leukaemia with PML::RARA fusion.
question: What is the most appropriate immediate action after morphology strongly suggests acute promyelocytic leukaemia?
options: Start all-trans retinoic acid (ATRA) immediately while continuing urgent confirmatory testing
Treat as standard AML with 7+3 induction first
Delay therapy until molecular subtype is known
correct_answer: B. Start all-trans retinoic acid (ATRA) immediately while continuing urgent confirmatory testing
explanation: APL is a medical emergency because early mortality is commonly due to catastrophic haemorrhage from DIC. Current practice is to start ATRA as soon as APL is clinically suspected while simultaneously obtaining rapid confirmation with FISH or RT-PCR for PML::RARA. Supportive correction of coagulopathy is equally critical.

why_other_options_are_wrong.A: Waiting for confirmation may increase the risk of fatal bleeding.
why_other_options_are_wrong.C: Standard AML induction alone is inappropriate initial management for suspected APL.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: Treatment should not be delayed pending transcript subtype.
why_other_options_are_wrong.E: Lumbar puncture is contraindicated in active coagulopathy unless clearly indicated later.

frcpath_pearl: In suspected APL, morphology saves lives. Notify the clinical team immediately and recommend urgent ATRA plus aggressive blood product support while confirmatory testing proceeds.
take_home_message: APL is the one AML subtype where treatment begins on clinical suspicion because delay can be fatal.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
